remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Dataiku named a Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for 5th consecutive year
remarks.content: For the fifth consecutive year, Dataiku has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for AI Platforms for Data Science and Machine Learning, once again recognized in the Leader quadrant for both “Completeness of Vision” and “Ability to Execute.”
remarks.contentTranslation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为2026年Gartner ®数据科学和机器学习AI平台魔力象限™的领导者，再次在“愿景的完整性”和“执行能力”的领导者象限中获得认可。
remarks.translation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为Gartner魔力象限领导者
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Lilian Weng's Blog (OpenAI)
remarks.role: AI research deep-dives, LLM architectures, agent systems, multimodal, alignment, safety.
remarks.text: Scaling Laws, Carefully
remarks.content: Scaling laws are one of the most critical empirical findings in deep learning. The observation is simple in form: the training loss $L$ decreases predictably as we scale up model size $N$, dataset size $D$, and compute $C$, following a power-law curve, which appears as a straight line on a log-log p
remarks.contentTranslation: 缩放定律是深度学习中最关键的经验发现之一。观察结果的形式很简单：当我们按照幂律曲线（在对数对数p上显示为直线）按比例放大模型大小$ N $、数据集大小$ D $和计算$ C $时，训练损失$ L $可预测地减少
remarks.translation: 谨慎地扩展法律
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: AI explainability in finance: auditable models, GenAI, and agents
remarks.content: Regulators are asking financial institutions a direct question: Can you explain how your AI made that decision? For most, the honest answer is no — not fully, not on demand, and not across every system that now influences regulated decisions. Compliance functions are expected to cover
remarks.contentTranslation: 监管机构正在向金融机构提出一个直接的问题：你能解释一下你的AI是如何做出这个决定的吗？对于大多数人来说，诚实的答案是否定的—不是完全的，不是按需提供的，也不是每个现在影响受监管决策的系统。合规职能预计将涵盖
remarks.translation: 财务中的AI可解释性：可审计模型、GenAI和Agent商
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: The AI success gap: why more AI doesn’t add up to more value
remarks.content: If AI is everywhere, why are so few companies getting real value from it?That is the question most enterprises are now facing. Eighty-eight percent of companies use AI, but only six percent generate meaningful value from it, according to a study from McKinsey.Pilots run, demos impress, a
remarks.contentTranslation: 如果AI无处不在，为什么很少有公司从中获得真正的价值？这是大多数企业现在面临的问题。根据麦肯锡的一项研究，百分之八十八的公司使用AI，但只有百分之六的公司从中产生有意义的价值。
remarks.translation: AI成功差距：为什么更多的AI不能带来更多的价值

Or do I know anyone who can work around corp bs?

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
As we are still investigating, I have reset everyone's Codex usage limits. This is a hard reset given some users had stacked up to three banked resets already that they can apply on their own schedule.

Funnily enough, this week at OpenAI is called the RESET week, which is meant for folks to relax a bit. However it will be a different kind of RESET week. Enjoy.
remarks.translation: 由于我们仍在调查，我已经重置了每个人的Codex使用限制。这是一个硬重置，因为一些用户已经堆叠了多达三个银行重置，他们可以根据自己的时间表应用。有趣的是，本周在OpenAI上被称为重置周，旨在让人们放松一下。但是，这将是另一种重置周。祝您玩得开心。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
As engineering, product, design, DS, etc. melt into a new kind of role, I was reflecting on what roles might look like in the future. For example, when I look at the Claude Code team I see what I think is five archetypes:

1. Prototyper: comes up with brand new ideas; churns out many ideas, most of which don't ship
2. Builder: quickly turns a prototype/idea into production-grade product/infra
3. Sweeper: cleans up the UI, simplifies the code and system, unships, optimizes performance
4. Grower: takes a product that has been built and iterates on it to improve Product-Market Fit
5. Maintainer: owns a mature system to make it secure, reliable, fast, and efficient as it scales

Many people span across 2 roles, and sometimes 3 roles. I also notice that these roles are not really tied to job function -- eg. across Anthropic, some designers match category 1, some 2, some 3; same for engineers, PM, DS.

A healthy team needs a mix of these, depending on the product:

- A product that is new and pre-PMF needs people that are strong at 1+2+3
- A product that is growing and has found PMF needs 2+3+4 and some 5
- A product that has strong PMF needs 3+4+5 and some 2

Maybe product roles of the future will look more like this, and less like the domain-specific roles of today?
remarks.translation:
随着工程、产品、设计、DS等融入一种新的角色，我正在思考未来的角色会是什么样子。例如，当我看到Claude Code团队时，我认为有五种原型： 1.原型设计师：提出全新的创意；产生许多创意，其中大多数创意不发布2.构建器：快速将原型/创意转化为生产级产品/基础设施3。
清扫：清理UI ，简化代码和系统，取消发货，优化性能4. Grower ：采用已构建的产品并对其进行迭代，以提高产品市场适应度5.维护者：拥有一个成熟的系统，使其在扩展时安全、可靠、快速和高效。许多人跨越2个角色，有时跨越3个角色。我还注意到，这些角色并没有真正与工作职能挂钩，例如
在整个Anthropic中，一些设计师匹配第1类、第2类、第3类；工程师、PM、DS也是如此。一个健康的团队需要这些的组合，具体取决于产品： -一个新的和PMF之前的产品需要1 +2 +3强大的人才-一个正在成长并发现PMF需要2 +3 +4和一些5的产品-一个具有强大的PMF的产品需要3 +4 +5和一些2也许未来的产品角色将看起来更像这样，而不是像今天的特定领域角色？
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text: Codex team is in a warroom on a Sunday combing through logs and checking whether there is anything that could lead to increased usage drains for some users. Taking it very seriously and won't rest until we get to the bottom of it. https://t.co/r7kYwqKjT2
remarks.translation: Codex团队周日在战场上梳理日志，并检查是否有任何可能导致某些用户使用量增加的情况。非常认真地对待它，直到我们深入了解它才会休息。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
How Anthropic PMs use agents internally to get closer to the product from Jess, product lead for Claude Managed Agents:

“Access to our codebase has been the biggest unlock for me.

It helps me manage state more easily. Rather than poking a bunch of engineers on what they’re doing, I can just track the PRs directly and see which ones are merged, which ones are deployed.

I deeply understand and interact with my product so much more than I’ve ever been able to in the past.”

📌 Watch the full episode here: https://t.co/rYIC7uyDpJ

https://t.co/8YMfGN06fl
remarks.translation:
Anthropic PM如何在内部使用Agent来接近Claude Managed Agents的产品负责人Jess的产品： “访问我们的代码库对我来说是最大的解锁。它可以帮助我更轻松地管理状态。与其戳一堆工程师他们在做什么，我可以直接跟踪公关，看看哪些是合并的，哪些是部署的。
我比以往任何时候都更深刻地了解我的产品并与之互动。”在此处📌观看完整剧集：

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
It should be 100% obvious that there will soon be mythos level models on cyber security that are open and available to anyone. As a byproduct of this, alternative tech stacks will emerge that also drive more economic value and control away from the US’s tech stack.

This is what should be considered when thinking through the gate keeping you want to have in AI. If advanced models will become open and available regardless, then by not allowing the release of models you’re neither more secure nor better off strategically.

So much of the regulatory approach to AI has to assume China can’t catch up, when all current evidence suggests they can and are. And further, hard to imagine a higher priority than winning in AI for China; so you’re basically betting against their long term ingenuity, talent and motivation. Seems like a bad bet.

So your options are either to create gates around your best models, which means you’re asymmetrically disadvantaging yourself, or you work to ensure you’re always at the frontier and driving the future architectures of AI.
remarks.translation:
很明显，很快就会有神话级别的网络安全模型向任何人开放。作为这种情况的副产品，替代技术堆栈将出现，这些技术堆栈也将推动更多的经济价值和控制权远离美国的技术堆栈。这是在思考你想在AI中拥有的大门时应该考虑的事情。
如果高级模型无论如何都将变得开放和可用，那么通过不允许发布模型，您既不会更安全，也不会在战略上更好。如此多的AI监管方法不得不假设中国无法赶上，而目前所有的证据都表明它们可以而且确实如此。此外，很难想象比在中国赢得AI更高的优先级；所以你基本上是在押注他们的长期聪明才智、才华和动力。
这似乎是一个糟糕的赌注。因此，您的选择是要么围绕您最好的模型创建大门，这意味着您处于不对称的劣势，要么努力确保您始终处于前沿并推动AI的未来架构。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。
